ddls:
CREATE TABLE "sentiment_data" (
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the sentiment data entry */,
  "democrats" REAL /* Sentiment score for Democrats */,
  "republicans" REAL /* Sentiment score for Republicans */,
  "media" REAL /* Sentiment score for the media */,
  "public" REAL /* Sentiment score for the general public */,
  "democratic_supporters" REAL /* Sentiment score for Democratic supporters */,
  "republican_supporters" REAL /* Sentiment score for Republican supporters */,
  "random_users" REAL /* Sentiment score for random users */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the source of the sentiment data */,
  "source_type" TEXT /* Type of the source (e.g., Twitter, Facebook, CNN, NYT) */,
  "region" TEXT /* Geographical region where the sentiment data was collected */,
  "sentiment_trend" TEXT /* Trend of sentiment over time (e.g., increasing, decreasing, stable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("date"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sentiment_data_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "political_groups" (
  "group_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each political group */,
  "group_name" TEXT /* Name of the political group (e.g., Democrats, Republicans) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the political group */,
  "leader_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the current leader of the political group */,
  "founding_date" TEXT /* Date the political group was founded */,
  "ideology" TEXT /* Political ideology of the group */,
  "website" TEXT /* Official website URL of the political group */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("group_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_political_groups_leader_id FOREIGN KEY ("leader_id") REFERENCES leaders ("leader_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_engagement" (
  "engagement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user engagement record */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the engagement */,
  "group_id" INTEGER /* ID of the political group the user is associated with */,
  "user_type" TEXT /* Type of user (e.g., public, Democratic supporters, Republican supporters, random users) */,
  "sentiment_score" REAL /* Sentiment score of the user engagement */,
  "engagement_count" INTEGER /* Count of user engagements */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the source of the user engagement */,
  "source_type" TEXT /* Type of the source (e.g., Twitter, Facebook, CNN, NYT) */,
  "region" TEXT /* Geographical region where the user engagement occurred */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the specific user who engaged */,
  "engagement_type" TEXT /* Type of engagement (e.g., like, comment, share) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("engagement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_engagement_group_id FOREIGN KEY ("group_id") REFERENCES political_groups ("group_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_engagement_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source (e.g., Twitter, CNN) */,
  "source_type" TEXT /* Type of the data source (e.g., social media, news outlet) */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL of the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "leaders" (
  "leader_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each leader */,
  "leader_name" TEXT /* Name of the leader */,
  "position" TEXT /* Position held by the leader (e.g., President, Leader of the Opposition) */,
  "tenure_start" TEXT /* Start date of the leader's tenure */,
  "tenure_end" TEXT /* End date of the leader's tenure */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("leader_id")
)
